yaml.title: Purposeful Program Theory
yaml.description: Book overview and chapter index for Funnell and Rogers' Purposeful Program Theory.

markdown.markdown:
::: {.landing-hero} Book notes Detailed study notes on Funnell and Rogers' *Purposeful Program Theory: Effective Use of Theories of Change and Logic Models* [@funnell_rogers_2011_purposeful_program_theory]. The notes are organized by the book's five-part structure and designed for later expansion into a multi-book evaluation knowledge base.
16 chapter notes 5 parts Theory of change Logic models
::: ::: {.section-grid} ::: {.section-card} Synthesis ### [Core Concepts](concepts.qmd) Review recurring distinctions such as theory of change, theory of action, logic models, assumptions, and causal inference. ::: ::: {.section-card} Map ### [Chapter Map](chapter-map.qmd) Use the part and chapter sequence as a reading map for the book. ::: ::: {.section-card} Practice ### [Evaluation Practice Implications](evaluation-practice-implications.qmd) Translate the book into practical habits for evaluation design, monitoring, causal analysis, and reporting. ::: :::
## Part 1: Key Ideas in Program Theory - [1. The Essence of Program Theory](chapter-01-the-essence-of-program-theory.qmd) - source PDF pages 30-41 - [2. Variations of Program Theory over Time](chapter-02-variations-of-program-theory-over-time.qmd) - source PDF pages 42-63 - [3. Common Myths and Traps](chapter-03-common-myths-and-traps.qmd) - source PDF pages 64-81 ## Part 2: Assessing Your Circumstances - [4. Scoping Intended Uses](chapter-04-scoping-intended-uses.qmd) - source PDF pages 82-95 - [5. The Nature of the Situation and the Intervention](chapter-05-the-nature-of-the-situation-and-the-intervention.qmd) - source PDF pages 96-121 ## Part 3: Developing and Representing Program Theory - [6. Processes to Identify or Develop a Program Theory](chapter-06-processes-to-identify-or-develop-a-program-theory.qmd) - source PDF pages 122-175 - [7. Developing a Theory of Change](chapter-07-developing-a-theory-of-change.qmd) - source PDF pages 176-225 - [8. Developing a Theory of Action](chapter-08-developing-a-theory-of-action.qmd) - source PDF pages 226-267 - [9. Representing Program Theory](chapter-09-representing-program-theory.qmd) - source PDF pages 268-319 - [10. Critiquing Program Theory](chapter-10-critiquing-program-theory.qmd) - source PDF pages 320-345 ## Part 4: Resources for Developing Program Theory - [11. Some Research-Based Theories of Change](chapter-11-some-research-based-theories-of-change.qmd) - source PDF pages 346-377 - [12. Some Common Program Archetypes](chapter-12-some-common-program-archetypes.qmd) - source PDF pages 378-413 - [13. Logic Models Resources](chapter-13-logic-models-resources.qmd) - source PDF pages 414-443 ## Part 5: Using Program Theory for Monitoring and Evaluation - [14. Developing a Monitoring and Evaluation Plan](chapter-14-developing-a-monitoring-and-evaluation-plan.qmd) - source PDF pages 444-495 - [15. Causal Inference](chapter-15-causal-inference.qmd) - source PDF pages 496-527 - [16. Synthesis and Reporting](chapter-16-synthesis-and-reporting.qmd) - source PDF pages 528-545 ## References
